linkedin.text:
The most honest measure of an event production company isn't its portfolio deck. It's how many clients book a second time.

At Uproduction Events, a significant share of our 1,500+ delivered events have come from clients who already knew what we were capable of — because they'd seen it firsthand.

Here's what I've noticed after 16 years in this space: decision makers at the director and VP level don't return to a vendor. They return to a partner they trust to protect their reputation in front of their own leadership and their own people.

That distinction — vendor vs. partner — shapes everything about how we design, communicate and execute.

For those of you evaluating event partners for the next planning cycle: what's the one non-negotiable you've learned from a past experience, good or bad? I'd genuinely like to hear it.

instagram.text:
Repeat clients don't happen by accident. 🔁

After 1,500+ events and 25,000+ participants across 130+ destinations, our proudest metric isn't scale — it's the HR and marketing leaders who come back to us year after year.

That kind of trust gets earned one event at a time.

Planning something? DM us. ✉️

#CorporateEvents #IncentiveTravel #EventProduction #B2BEvents #CompanyRetreat #TeamEvents #EventPlanning #CorporateTravel #HRLeaders #BusinessEvents

facebook.text:
The number that means more to us than any other: repeat clients.

After 1,500+ events across 130+ destinations, the clearest signal we get that we did something right isn't an award — it's when an HR director emails us before their annual planning cycle even starts, already knowing they want to work with us again.

We've built events for companies whose teams have grown, whose goals have shifted, and whose expectations have risen every single year. And we've grown right alongside them.

If you're planning your next company event or incentive trip and want a partner who already knows what 'excellent' looks like — drop a comment or send us a message. Happy to talk through what's possible.
